selected_important_turn.text: I do need more food than firewood, If I could get 3 food and 2 firewood. You can get 3 water and 1 firewood.
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn shifts the negotiation away from the opponent's earlier zero-sum demand ("all the water") toward an integrative offer that trades water for food and wood. It sets a concrete allocation that structures the remaining bargaining and anchors expectations for what each side might accept.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although constructive, the offer is presented as a fixed package without acknowledging the opponent's constraints or inviting alternatives. It risks anchoring the distribution and misses checking the opponent's minimums (e.g., whether they need any food) or offering multiple packages that could increase perceived fairness and buy-in. That reduces opportunities for a smoother, faster agreement and may leave unused integrative value on the table.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Frame this as a tentative proposal with brief rationale and an explicit invitation to modify it (e.g., "I can propose 3F/2W for me, 3Water/1Wood for you — would that work, or would you prefer a different split?"), or offer two alternative packages that satisfy your priorities while giving the opponent options. Ask a short question to confirm the opponent's minimum needs before locking the split.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (more potential integrative value captured by offering options and eliciting constraints)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (opponent feels heard if minimum needs are acknowledged and options offered)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (fewer rounds needed if you invite adjustments and offer alternatives)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase (shows collaborative, flexible stance instead of anchoring)

value2reason.Medium: Food is regarded as fuel and is needed to keep the body alive.
value2reason.High: Water will keep your body hydrated and will keep you alive longer.

value2reason.Low: It is hot and I have a fire starting kit.
value2reason.Medium: I can get by on small portions and forage in the area.
value2reason.High: It is very hot and I will need hydration.

dialogue.text: Doing good. How are you today?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm good as well, trying to plan my camping trip for the weekend. Do you enjoy camping?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I do but it's hotter than I expected. Probably gonna drink a lot of water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: It pretty cool where I live so I don't need many water. But I do need a lot of food and firewood. Have a huge family and we do a lot of grilling.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I would give you all the wood if I could have all of the water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I do need more food than firewood, If I could get 3 food and 2 firewood. You can get 3 water and 1 firewood.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm willing to trade 1 water for 1 firewood, but I need at least 2 waters to stay hydrated. Let's find a fair split.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I can propose 3F/2W for me, 3Water/1Wood for you — would that work, or would you prefer a different split?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm okay with 3 food and 2 water for me, and 3 water and 1 firewood for you. Let's confirm this split.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Let me double-check the split: 3 Food and 2 Water for me, and 3 Water and 1 Firewood for you. Does that cover all packages and meet your needs?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Agreed. Final split: 3 Food and 2 Water for me, 3 Water and 1 Firewood for you. Let's head out.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1
